prerequisites: An Elastic Email API key created in your account (configure the api_key field).
A start_date value to set the earliest date from which the connector replicates records.

authMethods.name: API Key
authMethods.summary: Elastic Email's v4 REST API authenticates with a single API key. The connector's api_key secret holds this value, which the API expects in the X-ElasticEmail-ApiKey HTTP header. Create the key in your Elastic Email account settings; the key is a GUID shown only once at creation, so copy and store it securely.

setupSteps.title: Create an Elastic Email API key
setupSteps.body: Sign in to your Elastic Email account and open the API Settings page under Account Settings. Create a new API key, optionally configuring custom permissions and IP access restrictions. The key appears once as a GUID, so copy and save it immediately because it cannot be retrieved after the window closes.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API key to the connector
setupSteps.body: Set the connector's api_key field to the API key you created. The connector sends it to Elastic Email in the X-ElasticEmail-ApiKey header to authorize REST API requests.

setupSteps.title: Configure replication and optional fields
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date field to the earliest date you want to replicate from. Optionally set the from field and scope_type to scope the data the connector reads.

sources.title: API Settings - Elastic Email Help Center
